title: Tempting Sweet Mexican Portobello Mushrooms Cochinita Pibil
description: A vegetarian twist on the traditional Mexican dish cochinita pibil, featuring sweet and tangy portobello mushrooms, this recipe is a crowd-pleaser and perfect for any occasion. It combines the flavors of plum jam and shio-cure for a unique taste experience.

ingredients.name: portobello mushrooms
ingredients.notes: stems removed and caps sliced 1/2 inch thick

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together plum jam, shio-cure, orange juice, and lime juice.
Add the sliced portobello mushrooms to the bowl and toss to coat with the jam mixture.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat and saute the garlic until fragrant.
Add the mushroom slices to the skillet and cook for 2 minutes on each side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 15 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the oven and sprinkle the chopped cilantro over the mushrooms.
Serve the mushrooms hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
